item.title: Two dead, including a 10-year-old child, in tragic Laucala Beach blaze
item.description: A devastating fire at Laucala Beach this morning has claimed the lives of two people, including a 10-year-old child. The National Fire Authority has confirmed the deaths to fijivillage News, and says investigations into the cause of the fire are ongoing.
item.content:encoded:
A devastating fire at Laucala Beach this morning has claimed the lives of two people, including a 10-year-old child.

The National Fire Authority has confirmed the deaths to fijivillage News, and says investigations into the cause of the fire are ongoing.

The blaze broke out at around 8am before rapidly spreading, destroying the residential property and extending to nearby commercial buildings.

They say the Police Forensic Team has recovered the bodies of the two victims, which have since been taken to the hospital for further examination.

The tragic incident has left the community in shock, with many gathering near the scene as emergency crews continue their work.

Stay with us for more updates as this story develops.

item.title: GCC wants a constitution developed by the people, for the people

item.description: Concerns have been raised over alleged incorrect road markings, lack of road signs and illegal parking in parts of Nausori Town.A

item.description: Villagers of Tamavua will soon have a state-of-the-art evacuation centre following the groundbreaking ceremony for the $1.78 milli

item.title: Naivalurua commends Beqa graduates for commitment to building a safer and stronger Fiji
item.description: More than 10 participants successfully completed training under the Solesolevaki ni Veitaqomaki initiative at Raviravi Village on Beqa Island and the Minister for Policing, Ioane Naivalurua has acknowledged their commitment. Naivalurua , who officiated as the chief guest at the graduation ceremony, thanked the graduates for their dedication and contribution towards building a better nation for fut
item.content:encoded:
More than 10 participants successfully completed training under the Solesolevaki ni Veitaqomaki initiative at Raviravi Village on Beqa Island and the Minister for Policing, Ioane Naivalurua has acknowledged their commitment.

Naivalurua , who officiated as the chief guest at the graduation ceremony, thanked the graduates for their dedication and contribution towards building a better nation for future generations.

He says the government recognises the commitment shown by the participants and their willingness to play a role in creating safer and stronger communities.

The Minister says the purpose of the initiative is to build, progress, sustain and achieve prosperity for the future.

Naivalurua stressed that this responsibility must be shared individually, collectively as a vanua, and as a nation to ensure a secure and prosperous Fiji.

The Solesolevaki ni Veitaqomaki initiative aims to strengthen community participation in promoting safety, security and national development.

The graduates successfully completed nationally recognised maritime training aimed at enhancing maritime safety, strengthening technical skills, and building the capacity of island communities to safely operate and maintain vessels while improving their resilience and self-reliance.
